title: Extract init plan model
description: Refactor `packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts` so current eager orchestration is split into a reusable install plan model plus executor primitives. Introduce typed plan/result structures that can represent preflight, scaffold, bootstrap, mcp, hooks, and post-setup phases before any writes occur.
test: Update unit/integration coverage so current init scenarios still pass through the new plan/executor path. Verify existing init tests compile and pass after refactor.
acceptance_criteria: A reusable install plan builder exists; eager writes are no longer triggered before execution; current non-wizard init behavior still works through the new abstraction.
hints: Reuse existing stage installers as executors || packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts;packages/cli/src/commands/bootstrap.ts;packages/cli/src/commands/config.ts;packages/cli/src/commands/hooks.ts
prev_context: [Explore state-machine] packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts already contains an implicit pipeline: preflight summary -> scaffold writes -> bootstrap/mcp/hooks execution -> summary output. The clean refactor seam is to extract a buildInitPlan() before any writes, then execute typed plan steps with richer results than the current ran|skipped|failed stage record. Existing bootstrap/config/hooks installers are reusable executors; the main risk is that initFabric() performs eager writes and must move behind confirmation.\n[Explore cli-surface] Current init surface is one-shot with skip flags, interactive toggle, and mcp-install scope. A breaking redesign should move toward plan-driven semantics, but T1 should preserve non-wizard behavior through the new abstraction.\n[Explore docs-tests] Tests and docs hard-code current init behavior, so T1 should preserve externally visible behavior while changing internal orchestration boundaries.
